name: Simonyan & Zisserman, 'Very Deep Convolutional Networks (VGG)', ICLR 2015

task.description: Create a self-contained 1920x1080 HTML slide titled 'VGG: ConvNet Configurations (Simonyan & Zisserman, 2015)'. The centrepiece is the famous dense configuration TABLE occupying the LEFT 62%: 6 columns 'A | A-LRN | B | C | D | E' with a dark header row, and rows grouped by block: 'input (224x224 RGB)', then conv blocks with cells like 'conv3-64', 'conv3-128', 'conv3-256', 'conv3-512' (deeper configs add rows: B adds a second conv3-64 and conv3-128; D is VGG-16 with three conv3-256/512 per block; E is VGG-19 with four), interleaved with 'maxpool' rows, then 'FC-4096', 'FC-4096', 'FC-1000', 'soft-max' rows. Fill at least 16 body rows so the table is genuinely dense. RIGHT 36%: a panel with four bullets ('Only 3x3 conv filters throughout', 'Depth 11 -> 19 weight layers', 'VGG-16 / VGG-19 most used', '~138M params (VGG-16)') and a small results table ('top-1 24.4%', 'top-5 7.0% (VGG-16)', 'top-5 7.3% (ensemble 2nd, ILSVRC-2014)'). Footnote at the bottom. Increase the configuration table to AT LEAST 19 body rows and add a final 'params' row giving each config's parameter count (133M,133M,133M,134M,138M,144M).
requirements: The configuration table has all 6 columns (A, A-LRN, B, C, D, E) with a distinct header row
The table has at least 16 body rows and no cell text is clipped or truncated
Empty/absent cells are still rendered as aligned table cells (the column grid stays regular)
The dense table is fully inside the left region and does not overflow the canvas
The right panel (bullets + small results table) shares the table's top and bottom edge (aligned)
No element overlaps any other element
Nothing overflows the 1920x1080 canvas; no scrollbar (the dense table must fit)
The four right-hand bullets and three result rows are all present and fully inside the panel
The footnote is fully visible above the bottom edge and overlaps nothing
Every text element is at least 11px
The configuration table has at least 19 body rows including a final params row, and every cell across all 6 columns stays aligned with none clipped
task.paper: Simonyan & Zisserman, 'Very Deep Convolutional Networks (VGG)', ICLR 2015

Key Features

  • Only 3×3 conv filters throughout
  • Depth 11 → 19 weight layers
  • VGG-16 / VGG-19 most used
  • ~138M params (VGG-16)

ILSVRC-2014 Results

Metric Error Rate
Top-1 Error 24.4%
Top-5 Error (VGG-16) 7.0%
Top-5 Error (Ensemble) 7.3% (2nd place)
Simonyan, K., & Zisserman, A. (2015). Very Deep Convolutional Networks for Large-Scale Image Recognition. ICLR 2015.

A dense configuration table on the left (62% width) with 6 columns and at least 19 body rows 2. A right panel (36% width) with bullets and results table 3. Footnote at the bottom 4. No overflow, no scrolling, everything visible Let me plan the table structure carefully: **Table columns:** A | A-LRN | B | C | D | E **Table rows (at least 19 body rows):** 1. input (224×224 RGB image) - spans all columns 2. conv3-64 | conv3-64 + LRN | conv3-64 | conv3-64 | conv3-64 | conv3-64 3. - | - | conv3-64 | conv3-64 | conv3-64 | conv3-64 4. maxpool (spans all) 5. conv3-128 | conv3-128 | conv3-128 | conv3-128 | conv3-128 | conv3-128 6. - | - | conv3-128 | conv3-128 | conv3-128 | conv3-128 7. maxpool (spans all) 8. conv3-256 | conv3-256 | conv3-256 | conv3-256 | conv3-256 | conv3-256 9. conv3-256 | conv3-256 | conv3-256 | conv3-256 | conv3-256 | conv3-256 10. - | - | - | conv1-256 Building the configuration table... trace.feedback:
Quality score: 60%
DOES NOT MEET REQUIREMENTS

Issues found:
  1. [CRITICAL] Configuration table has only 16 visible body rows instead of the required minimum 19 rows (at left panel)
  2. [CRITICAL] Missing final 'params' row that should show parameter counts for each configuration (at bottom of left table)
  3. [MAJOR] Some text in the configuration table appears to be at or below 11px minimum size requirement (at left table cells)

Suggested fixes:
  1. Add at least 3 more body rows to reach the minimum 19 rows requirement
  2. Include the final 'params' row showing parameter counts (133M, 133M, 134M, 138M, 144M) for each configuration
  3. Increase font size in table cells to ensure all text meets the 11px minimum
  4. Verify all table content fits within the canvas without requiring scrolling

trace.output:
The feedback says: 1. Configuration table has only 16 visible body rows instead of required minimum 19 rows 2. Missing final 'params' row 3. Text in configuration table cells appears to be at or below 11px minimum Looking at my previous HTML, I actually had 27 rows in the tbody including the params row. The issue seems to be that the table isn't tall enough to display all rows - they're being hidden due to overflow. The main issue is that the title bar takes 72px, the footnote takes 38px, and padding/gaps take space. The table needs more vertical space. Now I'm working through the math on the available space. With the title bar and footnote accounted for, I have roughly 913px for the table area. My 27 rows plus header should only need about 705px, so they should fit—but something's preventing them from displaying properly. I'm suspecting the flex layout is the culprit. The left panel might not be getting enough height from its parent container, or the table-wrapper's `overflow: hidden` combined with `height: 100%` on the table itself is creating a constraint that the flex container isn't satisfying. Let me trace through the hierarchy to see where the height calculation is breaking down. I'm realizing the core issue is that the table-wrapper is clipping content due to height constraints, so I need to recalculate the space allocation across the entire canvas to ensure all 27 body rows plus the params row fit. I'll trim the title bar down to 56px, reduce the footnote to 30px, and tighten the main padding to free up more vertical space for the table. I think the core issue is that `height: 100%` on the table forces it to fill the wrapper, which stretches the rows beyond their natural 25px height. The simplest solution is to remove that constraint and let the table size itself naturally, then handle scrolling with `overflow-y: auto` on the wrapper instead of `overflow: hidden`. But that creates a scrollbar problem if the table fits. Let me recalculate the available space: the left panel is 1080px total, minus the title (56px), footnote (30px), padding, and h2 heading, leaving roughly 950px for the table itself. With a 30px header and 27 body rows, I can set each row to about 34px and everything fits perfectly without needing to stretch or scroll. I'm realizing the core issue might be the `height: 100%` constraint on the table itself—let me set an explicit height on the wrapper instead. I'll calculate the available space: 1080px slide minus the 72px title bar, 38px footnote, and spacing around the heading leaves about 880px for the table wrapper. With that fixed height, I can fit the header row at 30px and distribute the remaining space across 27 body rows at roughly 31px each. Writing CSS fixes... Looking at the issues, the core problem is the table's `height: 100%` inside a flex container isn't giving it enough rendered height, clipping rows. The smallest fix: give `#table-wrapper` an explicit pixel height calculated to fit all 27 body rows, remove `height:100%` from the table, set row heights explicitly, and bump font sizes to 13px. ```html VGG ConvNet Configurations
